design_philosophy.core_loop: Match colored Puyos in groups of four or more to clear blocks and send garbage to opponent; chain multiple clears for cumulative damage.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time spatial puzzle solving under pressure; managing rising playfield while anticipating opponent's chain potential.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming a skilled puzzle combatant; outthinking and out-chaining opponents in competitive matchups.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Puyo Puyo is explicitly designed as a competitive puzzle experience derived from Tetris and Dr. Mario but with deeper strategic chaining mechanics. It mandates head-to-head play as the core experience (versus arcade competitors or computer opponents in story mode). The inclusion of narrative framing (Arle fighting 12 opponents before the Dark Prince) treats puzzle mastery as a narrative progression, not just high-score chasing. This elevates puzzle mechanics to story-driven combat rather than abstract puzzles.

difficulty.continues_system: Story mode progresses through fixed sequence of 12 opponents plus final boss; likely continues available between matches.
difficulty.checkpointing: Structured opponent sequence in Single/Story mode acts as progression checkpoints; endless mode for continuous play.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three mode selection (Single/Story, Double/Versus, Endless) allows player choice of difficulty pressure. Mission mode provides structured challenge scenarios with limited pieces.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode progresses through fixed 12-opponent gauntlet; beating the Dark Prince completes the narrative.
replayability_hooks: Multiple game modes (Single, Double, Endless, Mission)
Mission mode with 52 distinct challenge scenarios
Multiplayer competitive play against human opponents
Different opponents with implied varied AI behavior

world_building_techniques: Character-driven opponent sequence (12 unique opponents before final boss)
Clear protagonist role (Arle Nadja) with defined motivation
Antagonist framing (Dark Prince as world-threatening villain)
Character motivation diversity (some opponents friendly rivals, Rulue infatuated with Dark Prince)

boss_design.philosophy: Final encounter with Dark Prince represents narrative climax. Opponent sequence treats each puzzle match as a story beat; progression narrative frames increasingly difficult AI opponents.
notable_bosses: Dark Prince (final boss; rules over 12 preceding opponents)

danger_signals: Dark Prince's threat to take over the world
Opponents testing Arle before final confrontation
environmental_storytelling: Narrative framing establishes clear good-vs-evil stakes despite abstracted puzzle gameplay
